2605.23655.title: CVSearch: Empowering Multimodal LLMs with Cognitive Visual Search for High-Resolution Image Perception
2605.23655.paper_summary: High-resolution (HR) image perception presents a key bottleneck for multimodal large language models (MLLMs). While visual search offers a promising solution, existing methods struggle with the trade-off between coverage and efficiency. Visual expert-assisted search is efficient but prone to blind spots when proposals fail, whereas scan-based search guarantees coverage at the cost of computational redundancy and semantic fragmentation. To address this dilemma, we introduce CVSearch, a training-free adaptive framework that dynamically schedules search strategies via an Assess-then-Search workflow. Specifically, CVSearch first invokes expert-assisted search when global information is insufficient, and only triggers a novel semantic-aware scanning mechanism upon failure. Distinct from rigid grid partitioning, this efficient scanning paradigm incorporates Semantic Guided Adaptive Patching to decompose images into semantically consistent regions, effectively mitigating object fragmentation. Furthermore, we devise a Dynamic Bottom-Up Search strategy driven by a Visual Complexity prior to enable efficient and precise iterative exploration of local details. Extensive experiments on HR benchmarks demonstrate that CVSearch achieves state-of-the-art accuracy while substantially improving search efficiency. Code is released at https://github.com/liliupeng28/ICML26-CVSearch.
2605.23655.paper_summary_zh: 高解析度 (HR) 圖像感知對於多模態大型語言模型 (MLLMs) 來說是一個關鍵瓶頸。雖然視覺搜索提供了一個有前景的解決方案，但現有方法在覆蓋範圍和效率之間的權衡上存在困難。視覺專家輔助搜索效率高，但在提案失敗時容易出現盲點，而基於掃描的搜索則以計算冗餘和語義碎片化為代價來保證覆蓋。為了解決這一困境，我們引入了 CVSearch，一個無需訓練的自適應框架，通過評估後搜索的工作流程動態調度搜索策略。具體而言，CVSearch 首先在全球信息不足時啟動專家輔助搜索，並僅在失敗時觸發一種新穎的語義感知掃描機制。與僵硬的網格劃分不同，這種高效的掃描範式結合了語義引導的自適應修補，將圖像分解為語義一致的區域，有效減輕了物體碎片化。此外，我們設計了一種基於視覺複雜度的動態自下而上搜索策略，以實現對局部細節的高效且精確的迭代探索。在高解析度基準上進行的廣泛實驗表明，CVSearch 實現了最先進的準確性，同時顯著提高了搜索效率。代碼已在 https://github.com/liliupeng28/ICML26-CVSearch 發布。
2605.23655.author: Liupeng Li et.al.
2605.23655.authors: Liupeng Li, Haoqian Kang, Zhenyu Lu, Jinpeng Wang, Bin Chen, Ke Chen, Yaowei Wang
